text:
In a realm untouched by time, there was a small village called Evergreens where everyone had wild cats for pets. One particular wild cat chose Oliver.

Oliver was bold, often chasing birds into trees, earning him the title of Little Foxe. But he preferred company. He'd follow Oliver around, doing whatever he did.

One autumn evening, a storm brewed. Thunder rolled against the trees. Strong winds shook the wooden houses.

Oliver searched for shelter under the biggest oak. That's where you always found him come rain or shine.

Outside, the storm howled.

Then thunder struck.

And night fell.

Oliver slept soundly.

Come dawn, a soft rain fell. He rubbed his feathers against the oak.

It wasn't big. Just... fuzzy.

That's you.

And he's fluffy. Always fluffy.

So goes the tale.

Now, Oliver isn't a fox. He's a big rat.

Just like big animals sometimes like little company.

That's just one story
